segments.text: Peter: Hi, Shona.
Shona: Hi, Peter.
Shona: Have you made up your mind about which internship programme to apply for?
Peter: Yes, pretty much.
Shona: I'm entirely at a loss.
Shona: I mean, there are so many good ones.
Shona: Can you give me some tips?
Peter: Sure.
Shona: What is your top priority when weighing up these programmes?
Peter: Well, I guess I'd go for ones that can help me with the skill set I lack for my future career.
Peter: I still need a lot of practical training in dealing with statistics.
Peter: But keep in mind that you don't want to set your goal too grand, like drawing up a business plan.
Peter: You might end up nowhere.
Shona: I see.
Shona: So, I want to learn how to organise ideas and present them.
Peter: Good.
Peter: Now you've nailed step one.
Shona: Now what?
Peter: You must also consider your strengths for a suitable internship programme.
Peter: Otherwise, you might get rejected or end up with one that you are incapable of doing.
Shona: But I have no idea what I'm good at.
Peter: Communicate with people around you or whom you've worked with.
Peter: That's what I did.
Peter: I thought I was a good team leader, but many people I've worked with for various projects mentioned something else.
Peter: They admire that I could work around the clock to finish projects on time.
Peter: And indeed, I'm more focused and perform better when I'm pushed for time.
Shona: Good point.
Shona: So what do you plan to do next?
Peter: I'll visit the careers officer to help me with the school application form.
Peter: You know, the one we have to submit before applying to companies.
Shona: But it is unnecessary to fill it in.
Shona: It's only optional.
Peter: Really?
Peter: I'm glad I heard from you.
Peter: Then I'll ask the careers officer to look at my personal statement and give me some tips on polishing it up.
Shona: Don't you need help with your CV?
Peter: No, I'm good.
Peter: I have already learned to write it in class.
Shona: That's good.
Shona: So what kind of company did you choose?
Peter: I've decided to apply for an IT company.
Shona: Why is that?
Peter: Well, even though I've learned how the IT industry developed in the past from different lectures, I know nothing about its latest trends.
Peter: I'm prepared to work in an IT company and fill in the information gap.
Shona: I see.
Shona: What do you plan to do next term?
Peter: I think I'll stick to most of the optional modules I've chosen.
Peter: But regarding the language sessions, I'm doing poorly in Japanese.
Peter: I'd better drop it before wasting more time.
Peter: I think I'll learn Spanish instead.
Shona: Oh, I learned Spanish for a year.
Shona: I think you'll find it interesting.
Shona: Do you know what job opportunities these companies offer interns?
Peter: I've searched online and browsed some of the schemes.
Peter: Do you know SAIC?
Shona: Yes.
Shona: I heard they offer internship positions dealing with customer interaction management.
Shona: But I'm not quite sure what that means.
Peter: Well, the position offers the chance to become fully aware of customer value and keep in touch with customers to keep them highly engaged and satisfied.
Peter: These include activities such as PR, communications, marketing and customer service.
Shona: Sounds like loads of work to do.
Shona: What about Gemini Technologies?
Peter: It's an IT consulting company.
Peter: Although many employees say it's stressful working there, I want to apply for this one.
Shona: Why is that?
Peter: Actually, the company provides service to improve software architecture.
Peter: But that's pretty demanding as a team of software engineers has to provide detailed analysis and suggestions for required changes before the deadline.
Peter: As an observer and participant of a particular project, an intern can learn how to work to a strict deadline.
Shona: That's not my area of expertise.
Shona: But I'm sure you'll excel at this.
Peter: I certainly do hope so.
Peter: Have you checked out Deloitte?
Shona: Of course.
Shona: That's a renowned accounting firm, and thus its internships offer excellent learning experiences in this regard.
Shona: You know, things like examining a company's financial statements - the income statement, balance sheet and cash flow statement.
Shona: I want to give it a try.
Shona: But I might get rejected, so I need a backup plan.
Shona: Have you read anything about Kearney?
Peter: Yes.
Peter: It is another consulting firm.
Peter: It provides opportunities to reach out to other business partners and pay attention to networking.
Shona: Well, that sounds interesting.
Peter: I'm thinking about Vortex.
Peter: But I haven't got anything useful from its website.
Shona: I worked there last summer.
Shona: It's an international company with headquarters in Spain, so most of the managers here speak Spanish.
Shona: Thanks to the internship experience, I became fluent in it.
Peter: Right.
Peter: That's not for me.
